subject: Re: THANK YOU
body:
thought you might want to read this..In the summer of
1994 Time magazine's cover  story featured a
scientific tale about the genetic underpinnings of
human infidelity. In case you missed the punch line,
it goes something like this: Individuals' most basic
drive involves insuring that their genetic codes
survive death, that motivations to make a billion
bucks or to be the most wonderful person in the world
are really attempts to attract the "right" person with
whom to transfer genes into the next generation. Men
and women are by nature supposedly fairly promiscuous
apes. As is the case among species where males' body
size is greater than females,' men are innately
polygynous (87% of the 1154 known human societies
allow multiple wives) with the more "successful" males
broadly spreading their genetic code (the last
Sharifian Emperor of Morocco, Moulay Ismail, sired
more than 1,000 children). Women, on the other hand,
limited by their ability to generally bear but one
child a year, will do whatever it takes to guarantee
the survival of their offspring, including tricking
supportive men into raising the another male's child.



--- nadia marcinkova <nadja2102@yahoo.com> wrote:

> My father just sent me an email..he got the bike. I
> have never seen him this excited about anything. 
> It's great! Really, thank you.
>    
>    
>   He said he would like to repay you in some way, so
> if there is anything he could do, he is happy to
> help..
>   He wished he could at least thank you in English,
> so he sat home with a dictionary and wrote a message
> for you ;
>    
>   Mr.Jefry
>    
>   thank you very very much , it is fantastic and
> beautiful  present , I am wery glad . / deliriously
> happy / 
>   I wish you good luck
>    
>    
>     I thought it was cute..   
>    
>   But I don't know what he is suggesting by wishing
> good luck to you..  
>    
>    
>   I love you.
> 
>    
> 
> 			
> ---------------------------------
> Yahoo! Messenger with Voice. PC-to-Phone calls for
> ridiculously low rates.

thought you might want to read this..In the summer of
1994 Time magazine's cover story featured a
scientific tale about the genetic underpinnings of
human infidelity. In case you missed the punch line,

bucks or to be the most wonderful person in the world

whom to transfer genes into the next generation. Men
and women are by nature supposedly fairly promiscuous
apes. As is the case among species where males' body
size is greater than females,' men are innately
polygynous (87% of the 1154 known human societies
allow multiple wives) with the more "successful" males

--- nadia marcinkova nadja2102@yahoo.com wrote:

My father just sent me an email..he got the bike. I
have never seen him this excited about anything.
It's great! Really, thank you.

He said he would like to repay you in some way, so
if there is anything he could do, he is happy to
help..
He wished he could at least thank you in English,
so he sat home with a dictionary and wrote a message
for you ;

Mr.Jefry

thank you very very much , it is fantastic and
beautiful present , I am wery glad . / deliriously
happy /
I wish you good luck

I thought it was cute..

But I don't know what he is suggesting by wishing
good luck to you..

I love you.
